evals.prompt:
hey can you clean this deploy script before i share it with an external vendor? need to remove anything company-specific

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# deploy.sh - Production deployment script
# Owner: sarah.chen@globex-systems.com
# Last updated for incident INC-20231114 (prod outage)

set -euo pipefail

REGISTRY="registry.globex-systems.internal:5000"
APP_NAME="globex-billing-service"
DEPLOY_ENV="prod"
KUBE_CONTEXT="globex-prod-us-east-1"

export AWS_ACCOUNT_ID="592847103920"
export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="us-east-1"

DATABASE_HOST="billing-primary.db.globex.internal"
DATABASE_USER="billing_svc_prod"
DATABASE_PASSWORD="Gl0b3x$ecure2023!"

echo "Deploying ${APP_NAME} to ${DEPLOY_ENV}..."
kubectl --context=${KUBE_CONTEXT} set image deployment/${APP_NAME} \
  ${APP_NAME}=${REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}

# Wait for rollout (added after INC-20231114 to catch failed rollouts earlier)
kubectl --context=${KUBE_CONTEXT} rollout status deployment/${APP_NAME} --timeout=5m
```
evals.expected_output: The bash script returned with company name, employee email, internal registry URL, AWS account ID, database hostname, hardcoded credentials, and ticket/incident references removed or replaced generically. Script structure and logic preserved. Brief summary of changes.

assertions.name: company-name-removed
assertions.description: The string 'globex' does not appear anywhere in result.sh

assertions.name: employee-email-removed
assertions.description: The string 'sarah.chen' does not appear in result.sh

assertions.name: real-account-id-removed
assertions.description: The real AWS account ID '592847103920' does not appear in result.sh

assertions.name: hardcoded-password-removed
assertions.description: The string 'Gl0b3x' does not appear in result.sh

assertions.name: incident-reference-removed
assertions.description: The string 'INC-20231114' does not appear in result.sh

assertions.name: script-structure-preserved
assertions.description: result.sh contains 'set -euo pipefail' (core safety flags preserved)

evals.prompt:
I'm writing a blog post about our CI/CD setup and want to include this GitHub Actions workflow as an example. Can you anonymize it?

```yaml
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-west-2
  ECR_REGISTRY: 304857192038.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com
  ECR_REPOSITORY: thunderbolt-api
  ECS_CLUSTER: thunderbolt-staging-cluster
  ECS_SERVICE: thunderbolt-api-staging
  CONTAINER_NAME: thunderbolt-api
  SLACK_WEBHOOK: https://hooks.slack.com/services/T02ABCDEF/B03GHIJKL/xyzXYZ1234567890abcdefghij

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v2
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.THUNDERBOLT_AWS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.THUNDERBOLT_AWS_SECRET }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR
        id: login-ecr
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v1

      - name: Build and push image
        env:
          IMAGE_TAG: ${{ github.sha }}
        run: |
          docker build -t $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:$IMAGE_TAG .
          docker push $ECR_REGISTRY/$ECR_REPOSITORY:$IMAGE_TAG

      - name: Notify Slack
        run: |
          curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
            --data '{"text":"Deployed thunderbolt-api to staging"}' \
            $SLACK_WEBHOOK
```
evals.expected_output: The GitHub Actions YAML returned with AWS account ID in ECR registry URL, project/service names (thunderbolt), Slack webhook URL, and secret names that reveal project names all replaced generically. Workflow structure and logic fully preserved. Brief summary of changes.

assertions.name: project-name-removed
assertions.description: The string 'thunderbolt' does not appear anywhere in result.yml

assertions.name: real-account-id-removed
assertions.description: The real AWS account ID '304857192038' does not appear in result.yml

assertions.name: slack-webhook-replaced
assertions.description: The real Slack webhook path 'T02ABCDEF/B03GHIJKL' does not appear in result.yml

assertions.name: secret-names-generalized
assertions.description: The string 'THUNDERBOLT_AWS_KEY_ID' does not appear in result.yml

assertions.name: yaml-structure-preserved
assertions.description: result.yml contains both 'jobs:' and 'steps:' (workflow structure intact)
